message:
feat(storage): add defer_fts_triggers config flag to opt out of synchronous FTS indexing (ft-wk5fo)

Add an opt-in StorageConfig flag that lets deployments trade search
freshness for write-path latency on the output_segments table. The
default (`false`) preserves the behavior the storage subsystem has
always had.

## The problem

storage.rs:169/173/177 define three FTS5 triggers on output_segments
(ai / ad / au = AFTER INSERT / AFTER DELETE / AFTER UPDATE) that
synchronously maintain the FTS inverted index inside whatever
transaction is writing the segment. That's nice for "search works
instantly" semantics but expensive — every append_segment_sync call
pays for inverted-index page rewrites inside its own transaction,
and high-throughput workloads that batch thousands of segments per
second see measurable latency ceiling degradation against the
indexer's lock contention.

## The opt-in flag

    pub struct StorageConfig {
        pub write_queue_size: usize,
        pub defer_fts_triggers: bool,  // <- new, default false
    }

When the flag is `true`, the storage init path (inside the
spawn_blocking_storage closure that opens the connection + runs
SCHEMA_SQL) executes:

    DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai;
    DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ad;
    DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_au;

immediately after `initialize_schema(&conn)`. `IF EXISTS` is
idempotent — safe on both fresh DBs (triggers just created) and
existing DBs being reopened with deferred indexing for the first
time.

## The catchup contract (operator responsibility)

With the triggers gone, new segment writes are NOT automatically
FTS-indexed. Callers MUST periodically invoke
`StorageHandle::sync_fts` (or the Cx-first `sync_fts_with_cx`) to
catch the index up. The crate already has the machinery:
`fts_pane_progress` table tracks per-pane index watermarks, and the
`sync_fts_on_startup` engine handles batched / resumable catchup.
The deferred-indexing path simply piggybacks on the existing
catchup, rather than running it every write.

Operator-facing caveat documented in the field's rustdoc:
  > Default `false` preserves the immediate-indexing behavior the
  > subsystem has always had. Flip this to `true` only in
  > deployments that have a periodic catchup tick wired and can
  > tolerate search-freshness lag equal to the tick interval.

## Reversibility

Re-opening a DB with `defer_fts_triggers: false` after it was opened
with `true` does NOT automatically recreate the triggers (this
commit ships the opt-in direction only). A future follow-up can add
the reverse path if operator demand surfaces; for now, reverting
requires re-running SCHEMA_SQL or dropping the DB. Explicitly
documented in the inline comment so the asymmetry doesn't surprise
anyone.

## Beads

Minor metadata — ft-wk5fo tracked as the umbrella bead for the
deferred-indexing rollout plan. This commit lands the mechanism;
wiring it into the runtime config (tuning_config.rs + the startup
path) is a separate follow-up scoped on the same bead.

Tracks ft-wk5fo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
